outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Trump walks back Iran deal hype: 'Not in a rush' — deal slips as Iran disputes uranium terms
outlets.summary: After overnight 'largely negotiated' claim, Trump told reporters he's 'not in a rush' and 'time is on our side.' White House confirmed deal won't be signed today. Iran state media disputed uranium and Hormuz terms.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Iran deal hopes cool as Trump says 'not in a rush' and Tehran disputes uranium agreement
outlets.summary: Trump afternoon walkback contradicts morning's optimism from Rubio. CNN analysis: Republican hawks (Pompeo, Cruz, Graham) are attacking the deal while the White House pushes back, creating fractures in Trump's coalition.

outlets.name: NBC News
outlets.headline: Trump says he won't 'rush into' a deal with Iran as hopes of imminent agreement cool
outlets.summary: Officials signal progress but deal still fragile — Iran and US using same language to describe fundamentally different deal terms. Trump: leadership is 'disjointed.' No signing Sunday; next window likely next week.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Top Iranian official says 'no agreement' on Iran giving up its enriched uranium
outlets.summary: Iranian official contradicts Trump's 'largely negotiated' claim — no deal on uranium surrender. GOP hawks (Pompeo vs. White House): 'Not remotely America First.' White House: Pompeo should 'shut his stupid mouth.'

outlets.name: Breitbart
outlets.headline: Iranian President Says Tehran 'Ready to Assure World' Not Seeking Nuclear Weapons — but No Deal
outlets.summary: Pezeshkian claims Iran not seeking nukes — but Iran International and Fox report no actual agreement reached on nuclear measures or uranium handover. Tasnim: Hormuz will not return to pre-war status.

outlets.name: The Hill
outlets.headline: Senate hawks blast Iran deal; White House blasts back at Pompeo for comparing it to Obama-era agreements
outlets.summary: GOP fracture: SASC Wicker, Cruz, Graham oppose deal. Pompeo compared it to Obama-era deals. WH comms director Cheung told Pompeo he had 'no idea what the f**k he's talking about.' Trump-to-Rubio: don't rush.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Live: Trump says 'not in a rush' on Iran deal — Tehran disputes uranium, Hormuz terms
outlets.summary: After a day of optimism (Rubio 'significant progress,' Pezeshkian nuclear assurances), Trump's afternoon 'not in a rush' contradicted the morning. Iran state media: 'wide disagreements' remain, no uranium agreement.

outlets.name: Bloomberg
outlets.headline: Iran Deal Optimism Fades as Trump Steps Back, Tehran Disputes Uranium Terms
outlets.summary: Brent fell 5%+ on the week from deal optimism; afternoon walkback suggests oil price floor may hold near $103. Iran insists on uranium sovereignty. Deal possibly one week away at earliest.

outlets.name: Reuters
outlets.headline: Iran insists on retaining formal control over Strait of Hormuz as part of any agreement
outlets.summary: Iranian sources tell Reuters Tehran will not cede formal Hormuz sovereignty. The US demands toll-free access; Iran demands controlled-access. These are mutually exclusive positions the deal framework has not resolved.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Trump 'in no rush'; Netanyahu says deal must fully dismantle nuclear program; IDF: drone near Lebanon border
outlets.summary: Trump afternoon walkback gives Israel breathing room. Netanyahu (CBS 60 Minutes): conflict 'not over' until enrichment facilities dismantled and all HEU removed. IDF reported suspected aerial target near Lebanon border.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Emerging US-Iran Deal Suggests Trump Hasn't Forgotten — or Forgiven — Netanyahu's Push for War
outlets.summary: Haaretz analysis: Trump is sidelining Netanyahu as his influence wanes. Netanyahu accepted deal 'with resignation' per senior US official. Trump told him: 'You'll do whatever I want.' Israel's leverage over the war's endgame is gone.

outlets.name: Times of Israel (Briefing)
outlets.headline: Daily Briefing May 24: Why Israel could object to potential Iran deal
outlets.summary: Four Israeli objections: (1) nuclear file deferred to Phase 2; (2) ballistic missiles off the table; (3) Lebanon ceasefire bundled in; (4) Iran retaining Hormuz 'management.' Netanyahu sees each as an existential risk.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Qalibaf to Pakistan Army Chief: Iran has rebuilt its military — any new US attack will be 'more devastating'
outlets.summary: Parliament Speaker Qalibaf warned Pakistan Army Chief Munir that Iran has 'rebuilt its assets' during the ceasefire. Any resumed US attacks would be 'more devastating and bitter for America than the first day.'

outlets.name: IRNA / Fars
outlets.headline: Iran: 'Wide disagreements remain' — Hormuz will NOT reopen under US terms; no uranium deal
outlets.summary: Fars News contradicts Trump's 'largely negotiated' claim. Iranian official: no agreement on uranium surrender. Tasnim: Hormuz will 'stay under Iran's management.' Iran seeking Hormuz sovereignty guarantee before any nuclear concessions.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Has Iran agreed to give up its uranium stockpile? Here's what we know
outlets.summary: Analysis: Iran's ~440 kg of 60%-enriched uranium is the core impasse. Trump demands surrender; Khamenei's May 21 directive explicitly forbids HEU export. The two positions are mutually exclusive — no bridge yet in the MOU framework.
